rows.claim: Professional Superforecasters assessed natural zoonosis as the most likely cause (74%) and a research-related accident as second most likely (25%), while noting that disagreement persisted among forecasters.
rows.claim_id: Professional Superforecasters assessed natural zoonosis as the most likely cause (74%) and a research-related accident as second most likely (25%), while noting that disagreement persisted among forecasters.
rows.source: Superforecasting The Origins Of The COVID 19 Pandemic
rows.why_it_matters: This is part of the decision-relevant evidence base.

rows.claim: The observed pattern of early cases can be explained by non-residential infection locations and stochasticity without requiring major ascertainment bias.
rows.claim_id: The observed pattern of early cases can be explained by non-residential infection locations and stochasticity without requiring major ascertainment bias.
rows.source: No Evidence Of Systematic Proximity Ascertainment Bias In Early COVID 19 Cases In Wuhan
rows.why_it_matters: This is part of the decision-relevant evidence base.
